summary: Julia Evans announces the release of her strace zine, a printable guide teaching people how to debug programs using strace. Originally planned as a mailed zine, she instead made it available as a free PDF download. The post provides practical printing instructions for making physical copies and encourages readers to share it with friends, colleagues, and students.
key_points: The zine teaches debugging with strace in a physical, printable format
Available as both portrait and landscape PDFs, plus a screen-readable version
Designed for grassroots distribution — print it yourself and share it
Represents an early example of Evans' zine-based approach to technical education
money_quotes: I wrote a zine to teach people about how to debug their programs using strace
I was originally going to mail it out to people, but it turns out I'm too lazy to mail anything.
So instead, you can download, print, fold, and staple it yourself today!
Give it to your friends/colleagues/students to teach them about strace!
You can also read it on your computer. But printing it is better!
key_insight: Technical knowledge about debugging tools like strace can be made accessible and shareable through simple, printable zine formats.
